nodes.context: Historically one of the most influential Mafia clans in Sicily, from the late 19th century. Ruled in Ciaculli and Croceverde Giardini, outskirts of Palermo.
The Sicilian Mafia organization; members of the Greco family were important figures in it.
The ruling body of the Sicilian Mafia; Salvatore Greco was its first secretary, later headed by Michele Greco.
Mafia family from Villabate, led by Antonio Cottone, who mediated peace between Greco factions.
Mafia clan from Acquasanta area of Palermo; clashed with Grecos over wholesale market rackets in the 1950s.
Rival Mafia faction in the First Mafia War; clashed with the Grecos in the early 1960s.
Mafia faction from Corleone led by Totò Riina; allied with Michele Greco during the Second Mafia War.
American Mafia organization; Sicilian and American mafiosi met at Grand Hotel des Palmes in 1957.
American Mafia boss from Villabate; played role in peace negotiations between Greco factions.
The ruling body of the Sicilian Mafia, composed of leading mafia bosses.
A faction of the Sicilian Mafia from Corleone, known for its violent rise to power.

edges.evidence_quote: Members of the family were important figures in the Sicilian Cosa Nostra.
edges.context: Greco family members were important figures within the Sicilian Cosa Nostra.

edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore "Ciaschiteddu" Greco was the first ‘secretary’ of the Sicilian Mafia Commission
edges.context: Salvatore Greco served as first secretary of the Sicilian Mafia Commission; later Michele Greco headed it.

edges.evidence_quote: Piddu the lieutenant asked for mediation from Antonio Cottone, the boss of the Mafia family of Villabate
edges.context: The Greco clan sought mediation from the Villabate family boss Antonio Cottone to end their internal feud.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Acquasanta Mafia clan (Palermo wholesale market war in the mid 1950s)
edges.context: The Grecos and Acquasanta clan were rivals in a conflict over the Palermo wholesale market rackets in the mid-1950s.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: La Barbera brothers (First Mafia War in the early 1960s)
edges.context: The Grecos and the La Barbera brothers were rivals in the First Mafia War in the early 1960s.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Uneasy alliance with the Corleonesi (Second Mafia War 1981-1983)
edges.context: The Grecos had an uneasy alliance with the Corleonesi during the Second Mafia War (1981-1983).

edges.evidence_quote: They both participated at the Grand Hotel des Palmes Mafia meeting in October 1957 between prominent American and Sicilian mafiosi.
edges.context: Greco cousins participated in a meeting with American Cosa Nostra members in 1957, likely discussing heroin trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: Cottone ... still had good connections in the US, in particular with Joe Profaci, who came from the same village. At the time, Profaci was in Sicily and it seems he played an important role in the peace negotiations.
edges.context: Joe Profaci, an American Mafia boss, helped mediate peace between the Greco factions.

edges.evidence_quote: the Grecos were no longer part of the power structures of Cosa Nostra but restructured their organization to adapt to the new ways of organized crime
edges.context: The Greco clan was formerly part of Cosa Nostra but later left its power structures.

edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Greco 'U Minnuni', the son of Salvatore 'The Senator' Greco and Leandro Greco's cousin, who had been one of the members of the Corleonesi death squad during the 1980s mafia war.
edges.context: A member of the Greco clan was part of the Corleonesi death squad.
